quotes.text: He didn't even look up when I walked in. That's how I knew he was scared.
quotes.speaker: The Maltese Falcon (1941)
quotes.context: Spade narrating or confiding to Effie about a meeting with Gutman
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the inverted-logic read of body language is pure Spade, and the confiding tone with Effie is a relationship the film uses exactly this way. The giveaway is it sounds a little too much like hardboiled-fiction prose; Bogart's Spade delivers these beats more casually in person.

quotes.speaker: Casablanca (1942)
quotes.context: Rick Blaine (Humphrey Bogart) says this to Ilsa (Ingrid Bergman) as a recurring toast, most memorably at the airport farewell.

quotes.text: You smiled. That's the first time I've seen you smile since you got here.
quotes.speaker: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (1975)
quotes.context: Nurse Ratched to McMurphy during a seemingly calm group session, with quiet menace
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the line weaponizes warmth the way Ratched always does, turning an observation into surveillance, which is her whole method. The giveaway is it's perhaps too precisely sinister; her real dialogue tends to hide the threat even further inside institutional pleasantries.

quotes.speaker: Jurassic Park (1993)
quotes.context: Dr. Ian Malcolm (Jeff Goldblum) argues that the park's all-female engineered dinosaurs cannot truly be prevented from breeding or controlled.

quotes.text: She cried at the wedding. I thought that meant something.
quotes.speaker: Kramer vs. Kramer (1979)
quotes.context: Ted talking quietly to a colleague about his marriage falling apart
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the defeated, half-confused tone matches Dustin Hoffman's Ted Kramer exactly, and the detail feels like real misread-signal grief. The giveaway is it's almost too neatly retrospective, more fitting a courtroom drama than a domestic kitchen scene.

quotes.text: Don't touch the glass, and don't stand too close.
quotes.speaker: The Silence of the Lambs (1991)
quotes.context: A corrections officer warning Clarice before her first visit to Lecter's cell
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — procedural caution delivered in a flat institutional tone is exactly how that prison staff talks, and the line primes dread without over-explaining anything. The giveaway is it's slightly too clean and symmetrical for throwaway background dialogue.
